original_abstract:
To access architecturally complex natural products, chemists typically design customized synthetic routes tailored to a single target framework. In contrast, nature’s biosynthetic assembly lines often employ a more efficient strategy, using divergent intramolecular cyclizations of a common polyunsaturated intermediate to generate a diverse array of molecular scaffolds. Inspired by these biogenetic pathways, we have developed an artificial divergent assembly line capable of producing an unprecedented variety of terpenoid indole alkaloid skeletons. 

Our approach provides practical access to multipotent intermediates, allowing for the systematic diversification of skeletal, stereochemical, and functional properties without needing to simplify the complex structures found in nature. By leveraging three distinct modes of [4+2] cyclization and two types of redox-mediated annulation, we achieved divergent access to five unique scaffolds—including iboga-, aspidosperma-, andranginine-, and ngouniensine-type systems, as well as a non-natural variant—in only 6 to 9 steps from tryptamine. We demonstrated the utility and efficiency of this platform through the successful total syntheses of (±)-vincadifformine, (±)-andranginine, and (−)-catharanthine. Because natural products feature rigid, sp3-rich architectures that enable precise molecular recognition, this strategy offers a robust framework for creating high-quality screening collections to discover and optimize new drug candidates.
humanized_abstract:
Accessing the structural diversity of architecturally complex natural products requires the design of synthetic pathways tailored to the specific structure of interest. In contrast, Nature frequently employs efficient strategies to produce diverse molecular architectures. Herein, we demonstrate that nature’s strategy can be emulated to access an unprecedented structural diversity of terpenoid indole alkaloids (TIAs) using an artificial divergent assembly line. Intramolecular divergent cyclization of a common precursor polyunsaturated intermediate affords an incredible structural diversity of TIA skeletons.

Our strategy offers a general route to obtain multipotent intermediates, thus allowing for a combinatorial approach to assemble diverse structural features such as skeletons, sterochemistry, and functional groups while avoiding structural pre-organization, which is inherent in natural products. We accomplished by means of three distinct modes of [4+2] cyclization reactions and two redox-annulation processes to generate five new diverse skeletons like iboga-, aspidosperma-, andranginine-, and ngouniensine-types and one new non-natural type, within 6 to 9 steps from tryptamine. Subsequent applications of this protocol led to the efficient total syntheses of (±)-vincadifformine, (±)-andranginine and (−)-catharanthine. Given that natural products possess highly inflexible, densely packed sp3-based frameworks which facilitate precise protein/molecule interaction, this method is excellent for obtaining, in large quantity, high-quality screening libraries which are necessary for the lead discovery and follow-up optimization programs of drugs of the future.

document.output:
Accessing the structural diversity of architecturally complex natural products requires the design of synthetic pathways tailored to the specific structure of interest. In contrast, Nature frequently employs efficient strategies to produce diverse molecular architectures. Subsequent applications of this protocol led to the efficient total syntheses of (±)-vincadifformine, (±)-andranginine and (−)-catharanthine. document.input:
To access architecturally complex natural products, chemists typically design customized synthetic routes tailored to a single target framework. In contrast, nature’s biosynthetic assembly lines often employ a more efficient strategy, using divergent intramolecular cyclizations of a common polyunsaturated intermediate to generate a diverse array of molecular scaffolds. Inspired by these biogenetic pathways, we have developed an artificial divergent assembly line capable of producing an unprecedented variety of terpenoid indole alkaloid skeletons.
